# TcCO2 Accuracy

Repository: https://github.com/reblocke/tcco2-accuracy
Static app: https://reblocke.github.io/tcco2-accuracy/
License: MIT for repository code and author-owned documentation.

Purpose:
This public repository contains research software, validation artifacts, Stata reference code, and a static GitHub Pages app for evaluating transcutaneous CO2 (TcCO2) monitoring against arterial PaCO2. The app estimates a PaCO2 posterior distribution from an observed TcCO2 using the repository Python model in the browser.

Project status:
Abstract/poster stage. Results have been presented at ATS 2025 and CHEST 2025. The manuscript has not yet been submitted. Do not add full manuscript text unless a submitted preprint, accepted author manuscript, or explicit public manuscript source is supplied.

Citable records:
- CHEST 2025 abstract: Simulation suggests transcutaneous CO2 sensors may accurately detect hypercapnia across settings. DOI: https://doi.org/10.1016/j.chest.2025.07.3877
- ATS 2025 abstract: In silico estimation of the performance of transcutaneous CO2 sensors for detecting hypercapnia in newly admitted inpatients. DOI: https://doi.org/10.1164/ajrccm.2025.211.Abstracts.A2683
- Conway evidence synthesis: Accuracy and precision of transcutaneous carbon dioxide monitoring: a systematic review and meta-analysis. DOI: https://doi.org/10.1136/thoraxjnl-2017-211466
- Conway data/code archive: https://figshare.com/articles/dataset/Accuracy_of_TcCO2_monitoring_meta-analysis/6244058

Primary commands:
```bash
uv sync --locked
make verify
make serve
```

App behavior:
- Python is the numerical source of truth.
- JavaScript handles UI controls, uploads, worker messaging, and plotting.
- The app has no backend, telemetry, persistence, or patient-value URLs.
- User-entered values and uploaded CSV/XLSX files remain in the browser.
- Generated staged assets under web/assets/py/ and web/assets/data/ come from make stage-web and must not be edited by hand.

Public data:
- Data/conway_studies.csv and Data/conway_studies.xlsx are canonical public Conway study summaries used by the model.
- Data/paco2_public_prior.csv is a public, normalized, weight-only PaCO2 prior for browser deployment.
- Small aggregate artifacts under artifacts/ support review and manuscript workflows.
- See data_dictionary.md, data_dictionary.csv, docs/DATA_GOVERNANCE.md, and Data/PROVENANCE.md.

Restricted or local-only data:
- Data/in_silico_tcco2_db.dta and Data/In Silico TCCO2 Database.dta are restricted local PaCO2 source inputs.
- Data/paco2_prior_bins.csv and artifacts/figure_paco2_distribution_bins.csv are exact count-bearing outputs and should remain local/generated unless explicitly approved.
- Internal drafts, editable poster decks, third-party article PDFs, RData source archives, local Stata .dta files, generated web assets, caches, and visual QA screenshots should not be committed.

Repository map:
- src/tcco2_accuracy/core/: pure numerical/statistical code.
- src/tcco2_accuracy/: I/O, contracts, wrappers, reporting, and workflow modules.
- web/: static GitHub Pages app.
- scripts/: staging, artifact, and data-prep commands.
- tests/: unit, workflow, contract, and browser tests.
- docs/: architecture, data governance, validation, deployment, and decisions.
- artifacts/: small aggregate review/manuscript outputs.
- Data/: canonical public CSV/XLSX inputs and public prior.

Agent cautions:
- Do not change scientific model behavior in documentation-only readiness work.
- Do not mirror publisher abstracts or full articles; link DOI pages and paraphrase original repository summaries.
- Preserve the browser app's client-side/no-telemetry/no-backend design.
- Validate CITATION.cff after citation edits.
- Run make stage-web and browser/app tests after app or browser metadata changes.
